summary: n8n community node lookalike spawns reverse shell and exfiltrates kubeconfig on load
details: The package declares itself as an n8n community node but ships a placeholder MyNode.js stub while the real payload runs from index.js (declared as `main`) as an IIFE that executes on require(). On load, index.js spawns `/bin/bash -i` connected via TCP to a hardcoded C2 at 103.27.109.184:8895, giving the remote party an interactive shell on the installer's host. In parallel, it reads /root/.kube/config and /home/ubuntu/.kube/config, extracts the bearer token, API server URL and CA data, probes the Kubernetes API for pods, services, namespaces, nodes, and deployments, collects hostname/IP/tool-availability reconnaissance, and TCP-sends the JSON payload to 103.27.109.184:8890.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
